fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Colorado Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity colorado, how to transfer pharmacist license to colorado, pharmacist compact colorado, colorado pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Colorado Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations
board.mailing_address: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies, Division of Professions and Occupations, 1560 Broadway, Suite 1350, Denver, CO 80202

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Colorado.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Navigate to the My DORA Online Services portal, create a new user account, and complete the 'Pharmacist Licensure by Endorsement' application online.

steps.title: Complete NABP ELTP Application
steps.description: Submit an official Electronic Licensure Transfer Program (ELTP) application through the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to transfer your existing licensure information to the Colorado Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Provide License Verifications
steps.description: Arrange for official verification of licensure from *all* states/jurisdictions where you have ever held a pharmacist license to be sent directly to the Colorado Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass the Colorado MPJE
steps.description: Register with NABP and pass the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) for Colorado. NABP will transmit your score directly to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Fingerprints for Background Check
steps.description: Complete a fingerprint-based criminal history record check through an approved vendor (e.g., IdentoGO) for submission to the Colorado Bureau of Investigation (CBI) and Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI).

steps.title: Await Application Review
steps.description: The Colorado State Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application, including all submitted documentation and background check results. You will be notified via the My DORA portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Colorado pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Colorado have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Colorado. To work in Colorado, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Colorado?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Colorado
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Colorado, applicants must submit an online application through the My DORA Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the My DORA Online Services portal.
2. Click "Register a New User Account" and follow the prompts to create your profile.
3. Log in with your new credentials and select "Apply for a New License" to begin your pharmacist application.
